mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment above 5.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment above 5.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 104 signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production below 104 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.1 million signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts above 1.1 million are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month rise in industrial production signals a monetary easing cycle that boosts corporate profits and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment above 5.5% and a 2-month decline in housing starts below 1.1 million signals a weakening labor market and reduced supply that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5.5% and HOUST < 1.1 million for 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment above 5.5% and declining housing starts below 1.1 million are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 104 and a 2-month rise in housing starts above 1.1 million signals a weakening demand for goods and services and increased supply that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO < 104 and HOUST > 1.1 million for 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production below 104 and rising housing starts above 1.1 million are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.1 million signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts above 1.1 million are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
